title: Today's AI Brief: What’s Worth More Than Cash in San Francisco Real Estate? Anthropic Stock
summary: A look at the value of Anthropic stock in San Francisco real estate, AI-assisted diagnoses at Boston Children's Hospital, and more.
imageAlt: Image from source article for What’s Worth More Than Cash in San Francisco Real Estate? Anthropic Stock
imageCaption: What’s Worth More Than Cash in San Francisco Real Estate? Anthropic Stock
imageSourceTitle: What’s Worth More Than Cash in San Francisco Real Estate? Anthropic Stock

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm looking at some fresh signals in the world of AI. First up, it seems that in San Francisco, Anthropic stock is now worth more than cash in some real estate deals. According to Wired, the median house price in San Francisco is over $2 million, and last month, at least seven houses sold for $1 million over the asking price. But what's interesting is that Anthropic, an AI startup, is now a player in these high-stakes transactions.
Anthropic Stock: A New Player in San Francisco Real Estate

Anthropic's stock is apparently seen as a valuable asset in these deals. I don't know about you, but I didn't know AI startup stock could be a bargaining chip in multi-million dollar real estate transactions. It's a sign of just how hot the AI sector is right now. This development has me wondering what other unexpected ways AI companies will start to influence traditional industries.

Also making headlines is Boston Children's Hospital, which is using OpenAI technology to improve patient care and diagnose rare diseases. According to OpenAI, the hospital has used their technology to help diagnose over 40 rare disease cases. This kind of application of AI has the potential to make a real difference in people's lives. It's heartening to see AI being used in areas where it can have a direct, positive impact on people's health.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
